questions.question: From the figure, in which of the following vessel, the pressure of the gas is the highest. [Temperature and volume of the gases are the same in each vessel]. [Diagram]
questions.explanation: According to the ideal gas equation $PV = nRT$, pressure is directly proportional to the number of moles ($n$) when volume ($V$) and temperature ($T$) are constant ($P \propto n$). Since vessel P contains the highest number of gas molecules as shown in the diagram, it results in a higher frequency of collisions with the vessel walls. Consequently, vessel P exhibits the highest pressure among the four vessels.
